title: DebateReport
description: Canonical report schema for debate status summaries. Captures the full lifecycle of a debate: topic, trigger cause, participating models, per-round summaries with confidence scores, final result, and chosen action. Used by scripts/status-summary.sh for console output and auto-saved to .codex-collab/reports/. Single source of truth for all report generation and display logic.

report_version.description: Schema version, aligned with codex-collab release version.

generated_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when this report was generated.

session_id.description: The codex-collab session ID under which this debate occurred.

session_auto_created.description: Whether the session was auto-created from config defaults (true) or manually started via /codex-session start (false).

topic.description: The debate topic — either user-provided or auto-derived from safety hook detection content via scripts/safety-hook-topic.sh.

trigger_cause.description: How and why this debate was initiated.

source.description: Initiation method: manual (user ran /codex-debate), auto_trigger (safety hook caution+ triggered debate proposal), rule_engine (rule cascade triggered debate).

hook_event.description: The safety hook event type that triggered the debate (e.g., 'PreToolUse:file_write', 'PreToolUse:shell'). Present only when source is 'auto_trigger'.

hook_tool.description: The tool name from the safety hook that triggered the debate (e.g., 'Write', 'Bash'). Present only when source is 'auto_trigger'.

hook_risk_level.description: The risk level that triggered the debate proposal. Only 'caution' or higher triggers auto-debate. Present only when source is 'auto_trigger'.

rule_id.description: The rule ID from the rule engine cascade that triggered the debate. Present only when source is 'rule_engine'.

user_approved_trigger.description: Whether the user approved the auto-triggered debate proposal before it started. Always true for completed debates (blocked proposals are not recorded as reports).

models.description: The two models participating in the debate.

max_additional_rounds.description: Maximum additional rounds allowed beyond default. Capped at 2 per constraint.

rounds.description: Per-round summaries capturing each exchange between Claude and Codex. Ordered by round number.

items.description: Summary of a single debate round.

is_additional.description: Whether this round is beyond the default rounds (an additional round requested via 'continue' choice).

timestamp.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when this round completed.

position.description: Claude's core stance for this round.

agrees_with_opponent.description: Whether Claude agrees with Codex's position.

position.description: Codex's core stance for this round.

agrees_with_opponent.description: Whether Codex agrees with Claude's position.

divergence_score.description: How far apart the positions are (0.0 = identical, 1.0 = fully opposed).

trend.description: Direction of position movement relative to previous round.

final_result.description: The outcome of the debate after all rounds completed.

consensus_reached.description: Whether the two models reached consensus (both agrees_with_opponent = true or divergence_score < threshold).

total_rounds.description: Total number of rounds executed (including any additional rounds).

final_divergence_score.description: Divergence score from the final round.

final_convergence_trend.description: Convergence trend from the final round.

claude_final_confidence.description: Claude's confidence score in the final round.

codex_final_confidence.description: Codex's confidence score in the final round.

winning_position.description: The position that prevailed — either the consensus position or the adopted side's position.

has_code_changes.description: Whether the debate result includes actionable code changes.

chosen_action.description: The action taken on the debate result, derived from user selection in the 4-choice handler or auto-applied consensus.

choice.description: The action chosen: apply_consensus (consensus auto-applied with approval), apply_claude/apply_codex (non-consensus user selection), continue (additional round requested), discard (result rejected).

decision.description: Normalized decision category: accepted (any apply_*), rejected (discard), continue (additional round).

adopted_side.description: Which side's proposal was adopted. 'consensus' when both agreed.

applied.description: Whether code changes were actually applied to the workspace.

handler_status.description: Detailed outcome status from the debate-result-handler.

git_backup_ref.description: Git stash or branch reference created before applying changes (for rollback). Present only when applied is true.

summary_text.description: Pre-formatted summary text for console display, generated by scripts/status-summary.sh.
